name: Cranberry Orange Holiday Wreath
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Pecans, toasted and chopped into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Cranberry Orange Nut Filling; set aside.
2. In a large bowl, combine 1 1/2 cups flour, undissolved yeast, and salt. Mix in 1 egg.
3. Heat milk, water, honey, and margarine until very warm (125 to 130 degrees F). Stir into the dry mixture.
4. Knead dough on lightly floured surface until smooth and elastic (about 4-6 minutes).
5. Cover; let rest on a floured surface for 10 minutes.
6. Roll dough out to a rectangle measuring approximately 16 by 9 inches.
7. Spread filling evenly, leaving about 1/2-inch from the edges of the dough.
8. Roll up the dough like a jelly roll, starting from the long edge. Seal the seam.
9. Place the rolled dough on a baking sheet sprayed with cooking spray, seam-side down.
10. Form the dough into a ring and pinch the ends to seal.
11. Make 1-inch slits in the dough around the outer edge at intervals (every 2 inches).
12. Turn each section of the dough so that the filling is visible.
13. Spray the dough with cooking spray and let it rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in size (about 30-45 minutes).
14. Beat the remaining egg; brush over the risen dough.
15. Bake at 375 degrees F for 25-35 minutes or until cooked through.
16. Tent the dough with foil during the last 10 minutes of baking to prevent over-browning.
17. Remove from the sheet and place on a wire rack.
18. Drizzle the cooled wreath with confectioners' sugar glaze.
Cranberry Orange Nut Filling:
In a medium saucepan, heat cranberries, orange segments, and honey until boiling. Reduce heat and simmer for 5-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, or until thickened. Stir in bran and pecans; let cool completely.
Glaze:
Combine confectioners' sugar, milk, and vanilla extract in a bowl. Beat until smooth, adding more milk if needed.
